name: ICS Supply Chain Implant
description: Attack tree modelling a supply-chain compromise targeting ICS/OT environments through trojanised vendor firmware updates, engineering software, or hardware components
root_objective: Implant persistent backdoor in ICS environments via the vendor supply chain

nodes.title: Supply Chain Implant into ICS Environment
nodes.description: Insert a persistent backdoor or malicious logic into an industrial control system via compromised vendor deliverables

nodes.title: Compromise Vendor Firmware Update
nodes.description: Inject malicious code into PLC/RTU firmware distributed via the vendor's update mechanism

nodes.title: Breach Vendor Build Server
nodes.description: Compromise the vendor's CI/CD pipeline or build infrastructure to inject code at compilation

nodes.title: Trojanise Firmware Signing Key
nodes.description: Steal or forge the vendor's firmware signing key to make malicious updates appear legitimate

nodes.title: Compromise Engineering Software Package
nodes.description: Trojanise ICS programming/configuration software distributed to customers

nodes.title: Inject Malware into IDE Download
nodes.description: Modify the vendor's engineering IDE (e.g., TIA Portal, RSLogix) installer on the download portal

nodes.title: Compromise Third-Party Library
nodes.description: Insert malicious code into an open-source or third-party library used by the engineering software

nodes.title: Insert Hardware Implant
nodes.description: Place a hardware implant (e.g., rogue network tap, modified PLC module) during manufacturing or shipping

nodes.title: Intercept Equipment During Shipping
nodes.description: Divert or intercept hardware shipments to install implants before delivery to the customer

nodes.title: Compromise Contract Manufacturer
nodes.description: Infiltrate the contract electronics manufacturer to insert malicious components on the PCB

nodes.title: Activate Implant Post-Deployment
nodes.description: Trigger the supply-chain implant once installed in the target ICS environment

nodes.title: Command via Covert C2 Channel
nodes.description: Activate the implant through a covert command-and-control channel (DNS tunnelling, steganography in OPC traffic)

nodes.title: Time-Delayed Logic Bomb
nodes.description: Pre-programmed activation based on a date, process condition, or maintenance event trigger
